title: Penne all'Arrabbiata
subtitle: Angry tomato sauce with chilli, garlic, and flat-leaf parsley
sourceNotes: A Roman pasta dish first documented in print in the early twentieth century. Ada Boni's Il Talismano della Felicità (1927) includes an early version; the dish is now a staple of Roman trattoria menus and home cooking across Italy.

content.text: Set the pasta water on to boil. Warm half of {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}} of olive oil in a medium saucepan over a medium heat. Add the sliced garlic and the dried chilli flakes. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes, watching constantly, the garlic should be golden at the edges, not brown. Add the crushed tomatoes with all their juice and the salt. Simmer uncovered for 12 to 15 minutes until the sauce has thickened.

Cook {{penne-rigate}} of penne until 1 minute short of the packet time. Reserve a ladle of pasta water, then drain. Add the penne to the sauce with a splash of pasta water and toss over a medium heat for 1 minute. Remove from the heat, drizzle over the remaining olive oil, add the parsley, toss, and serve.
